sections.paragraph:
Νικολαΐτης, ου, ὁ Nicolaitan, an ethnic substantive (on the form s. Mussies 151) a follower of Nicolaus, an otherw. unknown founder of a sect, ἔργα τῶν Νικολαϊτῶν Rv 2:6. διδαχὴ τῶν Νικολαϊτῶν vs. 15. On the sect of the Nicolaitans s. ESchürer, Die Prophetin Isabel: Weizsäcker Festschr. 1892, 39–58; LSeesemann, StKr 66, 1893, 47–82; GWohlenberg, NKZ 6, 1895, 923ff; Zahn, Einl. II3 623f; AvHarnack, The Sect of the Nicolaitans and Nicolaus, the Deacon in Jerus.: JR 3, 1923, 413–22; MGoguel, Les Nicolaïtes: RHR 115, ’37, 5–36; NBrox, Nikolaos u. Nikolaitan, VigChr 19, ’65, 23–30.—Cp. DELG s.v. νίκη.

Mussies Mussies = GM., The Morphology of Koine Greek—List 6

otherw. otherw. = otherwise

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

StKr StKr = Theologische Studien und Kritiken—List 6

NKZ NKZ = Neue Kirchliche Zeitschrift—List 6

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

JR JR = Journal of Religion—List 6

RHR RHR = Revue de l’histoire des religions—List 6

VigChr VigChr = Vigiliae Christianae—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 673
